Year in Review

What Happened
in 1912?

Major events, music, film, tech & prices from 1912

The World in 1912
🌍EventsRMS Titanic sinks on maiden voyage; 1,517 die (Apr 15) · New Mexico and Arizona become 47th and 48th US states (Jan–Feb) · Woodrow Wilson wins Democratic presidential nomination (Jul) · Stockholm Summer Olympics open with 28 nations (May 5) · Woodrow Wilson elected 28th US President (Nov 5) · First Balkan War begins in October
🎵MusicWaiting for the Robert E. Lee – Heidelberg Quintet
🎬FilmQueen Elizabeth (film)
💻TechThe RMS Titanic disaster led to mandatory wireless radio distress protocols
🛒PricesBread $0.05 · Gas $0.24/gal · House $2,750

What major events happened in 1912?

1912 was a year of significant world events that shaped history. From political upheavals to cultural milestones, the year left a lasting mark on society. The music charts, cinema screens, and technological landscape of 1912 reflect the era's unique character. Prices for everyday goods like bread, gasoline, and homes give a vivid snapshot of the economic climate of the time.

Why is 1912 historically significant?

Each year carries its own defining moments and cultural touchstones. 1912 contributed unique chapters to the story of the 20th and 21st centuries. Whether you lived through 1912 or are researching it, the events, art, technology, and everyday costs of that year paint a rich picture of life at the time.

Frequently asked questions

What major events happened in 1912?

Major world events of 1912 include: RMS Titanic sinks on maiden voyage; 1,517 die (Apr 15); New Mexico and Arizona become 47th and 48th US states (Jan–Feb); Woodrow Wilson wins Democratic presidential nomination (Jul); Stockholm Summer Olympics open with 28 nations (May 5).

What was the #1 song in 1912?

The most iconic song of 1912 was Waiting for the Robert E. Lee – Heidelberg Quintet.

What was the top film in 1912?

The standout film of 1912 was Queen Elizabeth (film).

What did things cost in 1912?

In 1912, a loaf of bread cost $0.05, gasoline was $0.24/gal, and the average house was $2,750.

What technology milestone happened in 1912?

The RMS Titanic disaster led to mandatory wireless radio distress protocols

Source Wikipedia – 1912
